# UC-10.14.13 · OT Asset Risk Score Threshold Breach

## Description

Tracks cumulative OT asset risk from vulnerabilities and behavioral signals so operations can prioritize patching and isolation before compliance gaps widen.

## Value

Tracks cumulative OT asset risk from vulnerabilities and behavioral signals so operations can prioritize patching and isolation before compliance gaps widen.

## Implementation

Configure Splunk ES risk notables with OT Security Add-on scoring models. Align thresholds with corporate risk appetite and NERC CIP-relevant assets.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: OT Security Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 5151).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_security` `sourcetype="ot:security:risk"` ES risk framework outputs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Configure Splunk ES risk notables with OT Security Add-on scoring models. Align thresholds with corporate risk appetite and NERC CIP-relevant assets.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_security sourcetype="ot:security:risk"
| stats latest(risk_score) as risk by asset_id
| lookup ot_risk_thresholds asset_class OUTPUT max_risk
| where risk>max_risk
| sort - risk
| table asset_id, risk, max_risk
```

Understanding this SPL

**OT Asset Risk Score Threshold Breach** — Tracks cumulative OT asset risk from vulnerabilities and behavioral signals so operations can prioritize patching and isolation before compliance gaps widen.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_security` `sourcetype="ot:security:risk"` ES risk framework outputs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): OT Security Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 5151). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_security; **sourcetype**: ot:security:risk. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_security, sourcetype="ot:security:risk". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by asset_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where risk>max_risk` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **OT Asset Risk Score Threshold Breach**): table asset_id, risk, max_risk


Step 3 — Validate
Compare failed or disabled searches in Splunk to the **OT Security Add-on** configuration UI and the **Saved Searches** list so names and schedules line up. Spot-check one failure against `scheduler.log` around the run time.



Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bubble chart (risk vs asset class), Prioritized table, Single value (breach count).


## SPL

```spl
index=ot_security sourcetype="ot:security:risk"
| stats latest(risk_score) as risk by asset_id
| lookup ot_risk_thresholds asset_class OUTPUT max_risk
| where risk>max_risk
| sort - risk
| table asset_id, risk, max_risk
```

## Visualization

Bubble chart (risk vs asset class), Prioritized table, Single value (breach count).
